calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Writ Of Admin Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER BABAK ABEDI M.D.'s Hearing for Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

Petitioner Babak Abedi's petition for writ of mandate is denied. Per the independent judgment standard of review, the weight of the evidence supports the decision of respondent Medical Board of California to revoke Dr. Abedi's physician and surgeon's certificate. The record demonstrates that Dr. Abedi violated his probation on August 14, 2017 when he missed a biological fluid testing appointment. Dr. Abedi's claim that he was too sick to attend the appointment or contact his monitor lacks credence because he informed his work the same day that he could not appear. (RT I, 19:5-15; RT II, 42:13-17.) Dr. Abedi also violated his probation on August 18, 2017 when his diluted test sample tested positive for D-methamphetamine. Dr. Abedi speculates that the sample was tainted or that there was a break in the chain of custody. Dr. Abedi has not cited any competent evidence showing that the lab results or Dr. Kuntz's testimony regarding those results is unreliable. Based on these violations, the court cannot say that Board committed a manifest abuse of discretion by issuing an excessive penalty. The misstatement in the Board's decision that Dr. Abedi was convicted of driving under the influence in 2011 is harmless error. The record shows that the bases for the license revocation were the probation violations and not the 2011 incident.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
